scene.text: A man in a grey coat walks the same forty meters of pavement, turns, and walks it back. His shoes have worn a pale stripe into the stone.
Citizen T-77. Since Day Zero he re-walks his old commute, every hour, on the hour. The route was discontinued years ago. His file doesn't know where he's going. Neither does he.
He's not malfunctioning, Keeper. He's remembering with his legs. It's the only organ Day Zero couldn't reach.

outcome.text: Logged. ...Watch him one more second. See how he checks his wrist at the turn? He isn't wearing a watch. He never noticed. Come on — the Bureau's waiting.

scene.text: A door in a brick wall. No handle, no number, no hinges on this side. It is not on the district plan. The brick around it is cold. The door is warm.
It's not on any plan because plans are records and records are checked. Doors like this only exist where nobody has interpreted the wall recently.
...R-440 dreams a corridor of doors exactly this warm. You'll meet it later. Try to act surprised.

outcome.text: Logged. Don't knock. I mean it. Things that are warm are running, and things that are running can be woken. Bureau. Now. Please.

scene.text: A plaza. Ordinary foot traffic — until, all at once, two dozen strangers break into a run toward the fountain. Three steps. Four. Then they stop, look at each other, and walk on, embarrassed.
Every day at 3:14. Different people every time — commuters, kids, a records officer once. They run toward the same spot, and none of them can say why.
Something happened here, Keeper. Something big enough that the forgetting couldn't take the running, only the reason. The body keeps a copy the Archive can't audit.

outcome.text: Logged. K-902's file has a street full of people running toward a thing instead of away. When you dive it — remember this plaza. Come on.
